rationale: Y recalls the two most significant author-confirmed bugs from the human thread — the trust_remote_code default flip and the endpoint-restrictions-resolved-from-wrong-pipeline issue (correctly noting the latter is now covered by a regression test) — plus touches the hf-config caching/mutability concern. X only substantively catches the trust_remote_code issue; its other findings (docstring gaps, duplicate warning logs, e2e-only coverage) are plausible but don't map to ground-truth concerns and one (duplicate warnings) is weakly grounded. X is more structured and evidence-tagged (slightly higher actionability), but Y's broader recall of the real reviewer concerns outweighs that.
